pattern: Two heaps, one of free room numbers and one of busy rooms keyed by release time, simulate the lowest-room allocation policy while tallying how many meetings each room holds.
hints: Process meetings in start order, and at each step free up any rooms whose end time has passed.
If a room is free, the lowest-numbered one takes the meeting; otherwise wait for the earliest-finishing room and shift the meeting's window.

Keep a min-heap of available room indices and a min-heap of (release_time, room) for busy rooms; when delayed, the next room frees at its release time and the meeting's end becomes release_time plus its duration.

edge_inputs.description: tie on count returns lowest room
out-of-order starts must be processed by start time

stress.description: many meetings with unique random starts and small room count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, 6)
    count = max(1, size)
    starts = rng.sample(range(0, 500000), min(count, 500000))
    meetings = []
    for s in starts:
        dur = rng.randrange(1, 50)
        meetings.append([s, s + dur])
    rng.shuffle(meetings)
    return [n, meetings]
stress.note: unique starts as required; shuffled to test sort-by-start

complexity.time: O(m log m + m log n)
